Hey, welcome everyone. I wanted to go over the pre-sell questionnaire. So this is what you would want a prospect to fill out while they're in the waiting room. If there is no in the waiting room, then you can fill it out with them. The reason we do this, and it's incredibly important, is that it pre-frames the prospect to value what you're doing more and to bring their pain to the top of their mind. And so it just makes the sale easier because it reminds them of their priorities, and it does it in an unassuming way. If you can do this, squat down next to them and kind of fill it out with them shoulder to shoulder, it builds rapport subconsciously when you do that. And having little things like this creates a selling system so that somebody who's not as good as you can still sell. So you're gonna walk them through, get their name, what time of day. Now, since they're not actually working at the facility, this part doesn't really matter as much. It's just like, hey, what time of day's work best for you? They can just fill it out because they're gonna be working out on their own time schedule. Any vacations, yes or no? This is just information for you. How long do you think you'll take your reach or goal on your own? Forever, right? After you've reached your goal, it's maintaining important. They should say yes, if it is. All of this feeds into the sustainability concept. How long has this been going on your mind? Why is it important to you get back goals? So it's really taking through a lot of the pain questions that we normally be taking, and it really gets them thinking about why this is important to them, right? Is that what you want? Yes, will you weigh in weekly? Let's say by weekly. So you can monitor your progress and make adjustments during the challenge. Are you comfortable losing that? Please let's why you're joining. You wanna join this. Why do you think you're a good fit? Are you okay with that? Can you follow personalized training and nutrition plan? They should say yes, yes. What's your goal weight? All this stuff. Have you struggled with the past being consistent? Yes, eating right? Yes, and all of these things are gonna feed into how you're gonna make this sustainable for exercise, sustainable for nutrition, and then sustainable from the accountability standpoint, right? And all of this is so that when we explain that it's a credit only type of challenge, that we're gonna give them the opportunity to win their money as credit towards staying. It should all make sense because we're saying to see results and extend creative exercise is necessary more than a few weeks. You agree, right? And we're feeding into the fact that even in six weeks, no matter what, they still need longer results, right? If you had a trainer, if you had a coach, working with you, explaining exactly how to reach your goal. Do you think you'd better results working on your own? Trying on your own. Yes, 10% of the game is training, 90% is nutrition. So it's just as important to eat out, eat in a way that mirrors your exercise, right? And a reason I call it exercises is because for these people, that's really what this is. If you're looking over a long period of someone's life, it's not necessarily training for Gen Pop. It's really just getting them to exercise regularly. If you really wanna change their life. So if we design a personalized meal plan around your goal, do you think you'd be able to see results better than dining on your own? Yes, of course they would. And so this is what you're gonna fill out with them. It shouldn't take that long to go through it. You're just gonna clarify any questions that they have.
